headline: Lodestone Energy begins work on 31.5MWp solar PV plant in New Zealand
summary_80w: Lodestone Energy and Centralines have started construction on the 31.5MWp Central Hawke's Bay solar PV power plant.

headline: Hithium looks to replicate residential solution ARKVOLT’s Asian success into Europe
summary_80w: Hithium, an integrated energy storage solutions provider known for its leadership in long-duration energy storage, is now seeing strong momentum across its residential businesses and C&I. We discussed this with the company at the Smarter E Europe trade show in Germany last month.

headline: CHINT: Bridging AI infrastructure and energy transition through integrated power solutions
summary_80w: CHINT Global's Beibei Zheng discusses how the company is responding to Europe's dual challenge of AI sovereignty and energy security.

headline: Australia adds 9.1GW of new generation and storage in FY26 with battery storage dominating
summary_80w: Australia's NEM connected 9.1GW of new generation and energy storage to full output in FY26, more than double the result achieved in FY25.

headline: Australia moves to legislate net-generator requirement for large-scale data centres
summary_80w: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has said that large-scale data centres will be legally required to put at least as much energy into the grid as they draw from it, and that this supply must be renewable energy.

headline: Zambia signs contracts for 312MW constituency solar programme
summary_80w: The Zambian government has signed five contractor groups to build 312MW of solar capacity, with a 2MW solar plant in each constituency.

headline: Sol Systems reaches financial close on 123MW Illinois solar portfolio
summary_80w: US solar developer Sol Systems has reached financial close on its 123MWac Peoria Solar Portfolio in Illinois.

headline: The summer solar surge is here for many. The challenge now is not scale, but flexibility
summary_80w: Renewable energy generation is now accelerating faster than energy systems can absorb it, according to Schneider Electric's Frédéric Godemel.

headline: Qcells secures ‘first’ UL, IEC certifications for perovskite tandem tech
summary_80w: Qcells has become the first company to achieve UL Standards & Engagement (UL) and the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) certifications for silicon-perovskite tandem solar technology.

headline: Reaching the ‘ready-to-repower’ stage of solar PV project maintenance
summary_80w: PV Talk: Solclaris' Joe Miletic discusses the 'ready-to-repower' stage of PV project O&M and how it differs from the 'ready-to-build' stage.

headline: SMA Solar increases 2026 guidance due to ‘improved market conditions’
summary_80w: German solar inverter producer SMA Solar has raised its full-year 2026 financial guidance following its preliminary Q2 financial results.

headline: Unirac acquires Terrasmart solar racking business
summary_80w: US solar PV mounting systems manufacturer Unirac has acquired the solar racking business of Terrasmart from its parent company Gibraltar Industries, expanding its product portfolio into the commercial and industrial (C&I) and distributed generation (DG) segments.

headline: Array Technologies acquires wires, cables and BoS specialist Affordable Wire Management
summary_80w: Array Technologies has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire wire management, cable protection and balance of system specialist Affordable Wire Management.

headline: Suntech: Building Trust Through 25 Years of Solar Innovation
summary_80w: At Intersolar 2026, PV Tech sat down with Suntech's General Manager Mr. Yang Hao to discuss how a quarter-century of experience, combined with robust industrial backing, positions the company for the industry's next chapter.

headline: California batteries missing out on revenue due to suboptimal bidding strategies, Gridmatic analysis finds
summary_80w: Battery energy storage systems (BESS) operating in California's grid left approximately US$98 million on the table due to suboptimal bidding strategies, according to a new analysis from Gridmatic.

headline: Eos to provide energy storage for US Department of Defense ‘Golden Dome,’ releases preliminary Q2 financials
summary_80w: Zinc hybrid cathode battery and storage system maker Eos Energy Enterprises has announced a strategic partnership with the US Department of Defense (DoD) to “enhance the resilience of national defence infrastructure.”

headline: ‘It’s on the up now’: How grid fee clarity unlocked BW ESS’ German ambitions
summary_80w: BW ESS' executive director Roberto Jimenez discusses the company's recently-announced first Germany project, in Klostermansfeld, Saxony-Anhalt, which could be one of Europe's largest when completed in 2028.

headline: Waaree, India’s biggest PV module supplier, opens 5.15GWh battery storage assembly plant
summary_80w: A subsidiary of Indian renewable energy equipment and component manufacturer Waaree Energies has opened a battery energy storage system (BESS) enclosure factory.

headline: ib vogt wins approval for 480MWh battery storage site in Australia’s Wagga Wagga Special Activation Precinct
summary_80w: German solar and storage developer ib vogt has received state planning approval for the 120MW/480MWh Wagga North battery energy storage system (BESS) in New South Wales, Australia.

headline: Port of Newcastle cleared to safely stage grid-scale battery storage systems in New South Wales, Australia
summary_80w: The Port of Newcastle has become the first port in New South Wales, Australia, to be approved to safely store grid-scale lithium-ion battery storage systems.

headline: Alpex Solar seeks ALMM inclusion as 2.2GW cell plant nears production
summary_80w: Alpex Solar expects commercial production to begin in August 2026 at its 2.2 GW TOPCon G12R solar cell facility in Kosi Kotwan, Mathura, UP.

headline: Australia’s Transgrid opens pathway for 900MW of grid-forming battery storage as synchronous condenser costs surge 38%
summary_80w: Australian transmission operator Transgrid has opened a pathway for 900MW of grid-forming inverter-equipped battery storage to contribute to New South Wales' minimum system strength requirements.

headline: LONGi’s alloy metallisation rollout charts technological route out of PV industry slump
summary_80w: LONGi’s has unveiled a suite of new technologies intended to demonstrate how the PV industry can innovate its way out of its current malaise.

headline: NextEra, Dominion submit merger applications to US regulators
summary_80w: US utilities NextEra Energy and Dominion Energy have formally submitted applications to state and federal governments to merge their companies, creating the largest regulated power utility in the world.

headline: LONGi reports 35.5% efficiency for silicon-perovskite tandem solar cell
summary_80w: LONGi's crystalline silicon-perovskite tandem solar cell has achieved a power conversion efficiency of 35.5%.

headline: ISC Konstanz appoints Dr Gerhard Mathiak as head of module department
summary_80w: German solar energy research institute ISC Konstanz has appointed Dr Gerhard Mathiak as its head of its module department.

headline: SSI awards first traceability certification to Chinese polysilicon plant

headline: Oman finances 6GW solar cell, 3GW solar module manufacturing facility
summary_80w: Oman has announced US$1.7 billion for 105 projects and investments across a number of sectors, including solar cell and module manufacturing.

headline: ‘We’ll be producing heterojunction this year in the US,’ says SEG Solar CEO

headline: Navigating US solar cell supply: a framework for a fractured market

headline: Zero-E Australia secures 5.3.4A approval for 145MWac solar-plus-storage site

headline: Harmony Energy energises New Zealand’s largest solar PV plant

headline: Australia to legally require large data centres to become net-generators of renewable energy
summary_80w: Australia will move to ensure large-scale data centres are required to put at least as much green energy into the grid as they draw from it.

headline: Sunrun, FranklinWH expand BESS virtual power plant capacity in California, Texas

headline: Alfen and CATL agree 5GWh sodium-ion BESS partnership in Europe

headline: Thailand needs ‘many regulatory reforms’ of electricity sector to catch up with ASEAN leaders on energy storage

headline: EU ROUNDUP: Operators commission 682MWh of BESS in Germany, Estonia, Belgium, Denmark and Bulgaria

headline: Behind CATL’s ‘zero degradation’ BESS technology
summary_80w: We analyse recent details that have emerged on battery OEM CATL's zero-degradation BESS technology, which was first announced two years ago.

headline: Hithium on the European C&I BESS market and its need for a 4-hour solution

headline: Australia’s New South Wales grants planning consent for 3.6GWh pumped hydro project at former coal mine
summary_80w: Gloucester Coa has received planning approval from the New South Wales government for the 3.6GWh Stratford Pumped Hydro and Solar project.

headline: US DISTRIBUTED SOLAR ROUND-UP: PureSky, ClearGen and Aligned Climate Capital advance projects

headline: EU solar fleet avoided US$22 billion in gas costs amid Middle East conflict
summary_80w: Solar power saved the European Union €20 billion (US$22 billion) in gas imports between 1 March and 15 July, according to SolarPower Europe.

headline: T1 Energy seeking US PV component partners as Austin plant taps semiconductor talent pool

headline: Bluebird Solar secures 439.35MW module order from NTPC REL

summary_80w: US module and soon-to-be cell manufacturer T1 Energy is looking beyond wafers and cells to catalyse domestic production of ancillary components such as glass, frames and even pallets—while tapping semiconductor industry talent to staff its expanding operations.

headline: Qualitas Energy secures US$63 million for 117MWp Polish solar portfolio

headline: India’s CERC moves to unlock 15.7GW of renewable grid capacity

headline: BlackRock subsidiary agrees to acquire Summit Ridge Energy

headline: What does a bankable route-to-market strategy for battery storage in Europe actually look like?
summary_80w: With technology risk now largely removed from the equation, assessing how a battery storage project will perform across market opportunities is now the main object of financiers’ scrutiny, writes Ryan Alexander of enspired.

headline: Over 4GWh of battery storage added to Australia’s updated National Renewable Energy Priority List
summary_80w: Australia's federal government has added 11 new generation and energy storage projects to its National Renewable Energy Priority List.

headline: Pumped hydro costs revised up, battery storage costs down, as Australia’s CSIRO publishes final GenCost 2025-26 report

headline: Masdar hits financial close on 5.2GW/19GWh ‘round-the-clock’ solar-plus-storage project
summary_80w: Masdar has reached financial close on what it called the world’s .first gigascale 24/7 renewable energy project'.

headline: SoWiTec announces insolvency due to excessive debt

headline: Solar PV provides 25% of power generation across the EU in June

headline: EnergyCo, Transgrid sign deal to unlock transmission capacity for 3.56GW of renewables and battery storage in Australia

headline: VIDEO: FEOC and the Future of BESS Procurement, a webinar with Intertek CEA
summary_80w: Energy-Storage.news proudly presents this webinar, sponsored by Intertek CEA, examining the impact of foreign entity of concern (FEOC) restrictions on US battery storage procurement.

headline: The technology breakthroughs behind Huawei’s next-generation Smart-String Grid-Forming ESS Platform
summary_80w: Huawei FusionSolar’s new Smart String Grid-Forming ESS Platform, LUTERRA, is born from technology breakthroughs designed to drive customer success.

headline: Lazard: US utility-scale energy storage LCOS has increased since restrictions on Chinese cells came into force
summary_80w: According to Lazard’s annual benchmarking report, the levelised cost of storage (LCOS) of utility-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) in the US has risen, reversing declines reported in the 2025 edition.

headline: Thailand is creating a ‘flexible and competitive environment’ for energy storage market participation
summary_80w: New market rules will encourage investment in energy storage as a key component of Thailand’s national energy vision, according to the country’s energy ministry.

headline: Australia’s Queensland government reportedly shelves 20GWh pumped hydro project

headline: Turkey launches tender seeking 900MW solar PV, 1.5GW wind capacity

headline: Renewables still offer cheapest LCOE in the US as all prices rise – Lazard
summary_80w: Renewables are the lowest-cost source of new energy generation in the US, despite increasing costs, according to Lazard.

headline: The challenges and risks of AI adoption in the solar industry
summary_80w: David Moser of Becquerel Institute Italia talks about the complex challenges that AI brings to the PV value chain.

summary_80w: Update 16 July 2026: Energy Storage Europe sent a statement to Energy-Storage.news on the leaked plan.

headline: How Dahua is expanding into renewable energy with AI-powered solutions
summary_80w: We catch up with Dahua Technology, one of the world’s largest video-centric AIoT tech companies globally, about its expansion into clean energy technologies like EV charging.

headline: Battery storage investment at stake as Australia’s AEMC consults on minimum system load market rules

summary_80w: The financing will support the Government of India’s PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana (PMSMGBY) initiative.
